title: Delicate Peruvian Lamb Seco de Cordero
description: A traditional Peruvian lamb stew characterized by its tender pieces of lamb and citrusy flavor profile.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder on all sides until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb and set aside, leaving the oil in the pot.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped cilantro, dill, and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1-2 minutes, until the herbs are slightly wilted.
Add the lemon and lime juice, white wine (if using), beef broth, salt, black pepper, cumin, and paprika to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the lamb to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2-3 hours, or until the lamb is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
After 2 hours of braising, carefully remove the pot from the oven and carefully add 1 cup of the braising liquid to a small saucepan.
Bring the liquid to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced and thickened slightly.
Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of vegetable oil to enrich the sauce.
Simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened to your liking.
Shred the lamb with two forks and serve with the rich and citrusy sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with additional chopped cilantro and dill if desired.
Flamb8 with white rum or red wine (optional).
Serve hot, accompanied by boiled potatoes, rice, or crusty bread for a filling meal.
